nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reefs, lagoons, and intertidal zones in the photic zone; typical of tropical Indo-Pacific shallow subtidal habitat

decorPreferences: loose placement on rockwork or rubble

avoidWith: heavily grazing herbivores that may consume the specimen before it establishes

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Purple Gracilaria (Gracilaria corticata). Shows deep maroon/purple branching thallus.
Secondary product image from Mosaic Macros listing.

summary: A deep maroon-purple branching red macroalgae (Rhodophyta) from the Indo-Pacific. Sold by Mosaic Macros; maintains well under standard Gracilaria refugium conditions with medium lighting and moderate flow.
careNotes:
Gracilaria corticata, sold by Mosaic Macros as 'Purple Gracilaria' (also listed as 'Maroon Purple Gracilaria'), is a red macroalgae (Rhodophyta) in the family Gracilariaceae, order Gracilariales — the same family as the most widely kept Gracilaria species in the hobby. Its distinguishing feature is a deep maroon-to-purple coloration; Mosaic Macros notes it displays 'more pronounced ridges than related Gracilaria species' and shows 'a deep maroon/purple coloration when compared to the brighter red members of the genus.' The branching pattern is typical of the genus.

As a Mosaic Macros specialty entry, dedicated published husbandry data for this specific species is sparse. Water parameters are extrapolated from family-level Gracilaria care and are consistent with established sibling-species data: temperature is generally advised in the range of 22–26°C (72–78°F), salinity 1.023–1.025 SG, pH 8.1–8.4, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH — standard reef parameters confirmed across multiple trade sources for Gracilariaceae. These are the same values used for Red Ogo (Gracilaria parvispora, sw-algae-007), Pom Pom (Gracilaria hayi, sw-algae-006), Green Ogo (Gracilaria tikvahiae, sw-algae-013), Red Spaghetti (Gracilaria pacifica, sw-algae-015), Monkey Finger (Gracilaria salicornia, sw-algae-014), Thick Ogo (Gracilaria sp., sw-algae-003), and Flat-Branched Striped Gracilaria (Gracilaria mammaralis, sw-algae-042).

Lighting is expected to be medium intensity, consistent with other Gracilaria refugium species. Growth rate is anticipated to be fast, in keeping with the genus. The deep coloration may vary with lighting intensity — reduced light conditions may intensify purple pigmentation, while higher PAR can shift color toward red-brown in many Rhodophyta.

Propagation is by simple fragmentation, the standard method for all aquarium Gracilaria. Cuttings that detach or are trimmed will generally form new growth points when placed under appropriate conditions.

Mosaic sells it in 1.5–3" cutting-on-plug and 5"+ cutting formats. As with all Gracilaria, herbivorous fish such as tangs and rabbitfish tend to graze it. No copper-based medications should be used in systems containing this or any macroalgae.

Mosaic Macros is the sole documented hobby source for this species at time of research. Numeric parameters are genus-level extrapolations from the sibling species listed above; those values are well-established for the Gracilariaceae family. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: In captivity, propagation is achieved through simple fragmentation: portions of the thallus that detach or are trimmed will generally establish new growth when placed in adequate flow and lighting. Sexual reproduction via alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations occurs in the wild but is not a documented propagation path in the hobby.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Confirms scientific name Gracilaria corticata, deep maroon/purple coloration, and branching growth form with more pronounced ridges than related Gracilaria. No water parameter or numeric husbandry data provided on product page.

additional.name: Sibling species cross-reference (Red Ogo, Pom Pom, Gracilariaceae genus-level)
additional.notes: Water parameters (temperatureC, pH, kH, salinity) extrapolated from established sibling-species data for Gracilaria parvispora (sw-algae-007) and Gracilaria hayi (sw-algae-006), both Gracilariaceae. Parameters are standard reef values confirmed across multiple trade sources for the genus.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
